qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I continue to receive the "not enough hosts" error when trying to launch a virtual machine, especially if I require a specific availability zone?
qa_pairs.answer: If you still get this "not enough hosts" error, or there is some reason why you need a particular availability zone, send an email to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca and provide the cloud name, project name, the steps you have taken thus far, and the reason why you need a particular availability zone if that is the case.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include when submitting a ticket if my instance still won't boot after following the troubleshooting steps?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have followed these steps and still can't get your instance to boot, submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your username, project name, cloud name, and any information you collected during troubleshooting.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step if I cannot connect to my virtual machine or a service it is running?
qa_pairs.answer: If you cannot connect to your virtual machine, or cannot connect to some service your virtual machine is running, check the System Status page.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if the System Status page reports an incident on my hosting cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: If there is an incident on your hosting cloud, wait until the incident is resolved then try to connect again.

qa_pairs.question: What is the next step if there is no incident reported on the System Status page but I still cannot connect to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: If there is no incident reported on the System Status page for the cloud hosting your project, try to log in to the OpenStack dashboard for your cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the login links for different clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: Login links for the different clouds can be found on the Cloud Wiki page in the section “Using the Cloud”.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I cannot reach the login page for my cloud dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: If you cannot reach the login page for your cloud, verify that you have internet connectivity: Try to reach https://www.google.com with a browser, for example.

qa_pairs.question: If I have internet connectivity but still can't reach my cloud's login page, what should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Submit a ticket to the cloud queue by emailing cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, and project name, and the steps you have taken thus far.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I can reach the login page for my cloud but cannot log in?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are able to reach the login page for your cloud but cannot log in, please see the “Can’t login to Cloud” guide in the previous section on this page.

qa_pairs.question: Once logged into the cloud dashboard, what is the first thing to check if my VM isn't reachable?
qa_pairs.answer: Navigate to the Instances screen on the left-side menu. Look at the Power State for your VM. It should be “Running”.

qa_pairs.question: What if my VM's Power State is not "Running" (e.g., "Shut Down")?
qa_pairs.answer: If it is not in the “Running” state (for example, “Shut Down”) try to restart it using the "Actions" menu on the right-hand side. Select "Start Instance" or "Resume Instance" depending on what options are available to you.

qa_pairs.question: How can I find out why my VM was taken out of the running state?
qa_pairs.answer: From the Instances screen, click on your Instance name (VM name) and then click on the “Action Log” tab. This will show all the actions that have been applied to your VM.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I find an unrecognized action in my VM's action log?
qa_pairs.answer: If there is an action you don’t recognize, contact support (email: cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca) for help to figure out what it was. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, and the User ID from the action log for the action you want to investigate.

qa_pairs.question: Besides the action logs, where else can I look for error messages related to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: The "log" tab from the same screen will show you the console log for your VM. Look at that for error messages as well.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I provide if I cannot restart my virtual machine and need to submit a ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: If you can't restart your VM, submit a ticket to the cloud queue by emailing cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, VM ID, the issue you are seeing, and the steps you have taken to trouble-shoot it so far.

qa_pairs.question: How can I find the VM ID to include in a support ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find the VM ID by clicking on your instance, then looking at the Overview tab.

qa_pairs.question: If my VM is running but I still can't reach my application or web service, what is the next troubleshooting step?
qa_pairs.answer: If you can’t reach your application or web service hosted on your VM, but you have followed steps 1-4 and your VM is running, then try to connect using SSH.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions for connecting to my VM using SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find instructions for doing this in the Cloud Quick Start Guide, near the bottom of the page at "Connecting to your VM with SSH".

qa_pairs.question: If I get an SSH login prompt, what should I verify?
qa_pairs.answer: If you get a login prompt, verify you are using the correct key pair and username.

qa_pairs.question: How do I check which key pair I should be using for my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: To check which key pair you should be using, click on "Compute" on the left-side menu of the OpenStack page, then "Instances", then look for the column “Key Pair”.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default usernames for SSH based on the VM's operating system?
qa_pairs.answer: The correct username depends on the operating system of your VM: Debian uses 'debian', Ubuntu uses 'ubuntu', AlmaLinux uses 'almalinux', CentOS uses 'centos', and Fedora uses 'fedora'.

qa_pairs.question: What if I changed my username using a custom CloudInit script?
qa_pairs.answer: If you explicitly changed your username with a custom CloudInit script, then the correct username will be what you changed it to.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I do not get an SSH login prompt?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not get a login prompt, check your security settings.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first security check to perform if I can't get an SSH login prompt?
qa_pairs.answer: Verify that your own IP address has not changed. Check your IP address by opening this link in a browser: https://ipv4.icanhazip.com/.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to verify my IP address when troubleshooting SSH connection issues?
qa_pairs.answer: Your IP address must be allowed in the security settings in order for you to reach your VM.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my IP address has changed and I can't connect via SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: If your IP address has changed, add a new rule to your Security Group.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check if my IP address is unblocked for SSH connections to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on “Network” in the left-hand side navigation panel, then "Security groups". Find the security group for your VM and click "Manage Rules". There should be a rule there with Direction "Ingress", IP Protocol "TCP", and Port Range "22 (SSH)", with Remote IP Prefix your-ip-address/32.

qa_pairs.question: How do I add an SSH rule to a Security Group?
qa_pairs.answer: Click add rule, select SSH from the list, then enter your-ip-address/32 in the CIDR field box at the bottom and click "Add".

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have completed all SSH troubleshooting steps and still cannot connect to my instance?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have completed all these steps and still cannot connect to your instance, submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca and provide the cloud name, project name, instance UUID, and all information collected from the above steps.

qa_pairs.question: How do I find the instance UUID needed for a support ticket when I can't connect to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: To find the instance UUID, click on "Instances" in the left-hand side navigation panel, then on the specific instance name you are having trouble with, then the Overview tab for that instance, and look for the "ID" field. The UUID will be a long string of letters, numbers, and hyphens.
